# Test mcp-probe + mcp-shadow + mcpdiff integration
# This test verifies that mcp-shadow can properly forward traffic between probe and servers,
# and that mcpdiff can analyze the resulting trace

# Build required tools
exec go build -o mcp-probe ../../../cmd/mcp-probe
exec go build -o mcp-shadow ../mcp-shadow
exec go build -o mcpdiff ../mcpdiff
exec go build -o minimal_server ../../../cmd/mcp-probe/minimal_server.go

# Create a temporary trace file
exec mkdir -p tmp
setenv TRACE_FILE tmp/shadow-test.mcp

# Test 1: Run mcp-probe through mcp-shadow to generate trace
# mcp-probe will output sample requests which we'll pipe through shadow
exec ./mcp-probe
stdout '{"ID":{},"Method":"initialize"'
stdout '{"ID":{},"Method":"tools/call"'

# Now run the same output through mcp-shadow with primary and shadow servers
exec sh -c './mcp-probe | ./mcp-shadow -primary "./minimal_server" -shadow "./minimal_server" -compare -o $TRACE_FILE -v'
stdout 'Server initialized successfully'
stdout 'minimal-server'

# Verify trace file was created
exec test -f tmp/shadow-test.mcp

# Test 2: Use mcpdiff to analyze the trace
exec ./mcpdiff -v tmp/shadow-test.mcp
stdout 'Primary'
! stderr 'error'

# Test 3: Check compare mode output
exec sh -c 'echo ''{"ID":{},"Method":"initialize","Params":{}}'' | ./mcp-shadow -primary "./minimal_server" -shadow "./minimal_server" -compare -o tmp/compare.mcp'

# Verify mcpdiff can parse the compare output  
exec ./mcpdiff tmp/compare.mcp
! stderr 'error'

# Test 4: Test with timeout scenarios
exec sh -c 'echo ''{"ID":{},"Method":"test"}'' | ./mcp-shadow -primary "./minimal_server" -shadow "sleep 10" -timeout=100ms -o tmp/timeout.mcp'

# Cleanup
exec rm -rf tmp
